The relationship between the fare η and the driving distance ξ can be expressed as a piecewise function based on the given conditions. For distances ξ ≤ 4km the fare remains constant at 10 yuan. For distances ξ > 4km the fare increases by 2 yuan for each additional kilometer. If the distance exceeds 4km by x kilometers then the total fare η is 10 + 2x. However since fractions of a kilometer are rounded up the fare calculation must account for this rounding. The fare η is thus a function of the rounded up value of ξ minus 4. This relationship ensures that any extra distance or stopping time is converted into additional fare. The stopping time is converted into driving distance by the rule that every 5 minutes of stopping time is equivalent to 1km. This conversion ensures that the fare η is directly influenced by both the actual driving distance and the converted stopping time. Spring is a season that brings change and in this context it reflects the variability in driving distance and stopping time. Spring also symbolizes the beginning of a new cycle and in this case it represents the start of a shuttle trip between the airport and the hotel. The fare η is therefore not only dependent on the physical distance but also on the time spent stopping which is converted into distance. This makes η a random variable that fluctuates based on the driver’s route and the time spent at stops. Spring is a time of renewal and in this scenario it highlights the dynamic nature of the taxi fare system. The relationship between η and ξ is essential for understanding how the fare is determined in this city.

The fare η is calculated using the formula 10 + 2(x) where x is the number of kilometers exceeded beyond 4km and rounded up. If a passenger paid 38 yuan and the actual distance traveled was 15km then we can calculate the extra distance that contributed to the fare. The base fare is 10 yuan for the first 4km. The remaining 11km would be charged at 2 yuan per km resulting in 22 yuan. This matches the total fare of 38 yuan. However the actual distance was 15km which means the extra distance is 11km. If the taxi traveled 15km then the extra distance is 11km which includes both the actual distance and the converted stopping time.
